name: Dulles International Airport (IAD)
content:
I have spent many a sleep deprived morning in Dulles International. IAD is very hard to navigate when your brain doesn’t work. The buses (which really look nothing like buses) that shuttle passengers between terminals are behemoths to behold. I have never quite figured out the lettering system for the buses, where they depart from, or where they go to, despite the fact that I have regularly used them. The sleep deprivation likely plays some role in my confusion.
The PenFed tunnel that connects Terminal C to the rest of the airport is a formidable foe. If you must enter or exit Terminal C, you must pass through a tunnel where a PenFed jingle will play continuously on loop at least three times before you can exit the tunnel, no matter how fast you walk. Apparently the airport got too many complaints at one point and switched to a different jingle (I honestly couldn’t tell the difference), but then switched back to the original jingle after one of my friends, knowing how much I hated the PenFed tunnel, emailed the airport and asked them to switch back to the original right before I had a layover at IAD. Later that day, that friend then proceeded to pick me up from my destination airport and immediately started playing the PenFed jingle during the car ride home. I am unsure of how I am still alive. Bring earplugs.
When it comes to food, I have a tradition of eating an Annie’s Pretzel every time I visit IAD, though you are free to come up with your own traditions.
